name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
description: PCI DSS v3.2.1 (May 2018) was a minor revision of v3.2 that updated SSL/early-TLS deprecation dates and migration timing. It comprises 12 top-level Requirements organized into 6 goals (Build and Maintain a Secure Network, Protect Cardholder Data, Maintain a Vulnerability Management Program, Implement Strong Access Control Measures, Regularly Monitor and Test Networks, Maintain an Information Security Policy) plus appendices A1 (Shared Hosting Providers), A2 (SSL/TLS migration), and A3 (Designated Entities Supplemental Validation). v3.2.1 uses the older 3-column requirement table format (Requirements / Testing Procedures / Guidance) — v4.0 later restructured this as Defined Approach Requirements / Defined Approach Testing Procedures / Purpose, plus added the Customized Approach.
relationToCloudSecurity: PCI DSS v3.2.1 was the active standard for cardholder data protection from May 2018 through Q1 2024 (when v4.0 became required). Cloud service providers serving payment-card-handling tenants commonly mapped their controls to v3.2.1 requirements. v3.2.1 requirement IDs are extensively referenced in compliance documentation pre-dating v4.0.
relationToAISecurity: PCI DSS v3.2.1 does not address AI; AI systems handling cardholder data were still subject to its requirements.

license.notes: PCI SSC License Agreement applies (same as for v4.0/v4.0.1). Free Read and Copy License for study purposes; modification prohibited.

links.textUsedNotes: PCI SSC retired v3.2.1 from public download. The authentic PDF (Author=PCI SSC in metadata, 139 pages) is hosted by University of Western Ontario at the URL above. PCI SSC's own legacy URL https://www.pcisecuritystandards.org/pdfs/PCI-DSS-3.2.1-Release.pdf serves only the 1-page press release, not the full standard.

lifecycle.supersededBy: PCI DSS v4.0 (March 2022) and subsequently v4.0.1 (June 2024)
lifecycle.notes: v3.2.1 was retired by PCI SSC on 2024-03-31 (the cutover date when v4.0 became required). The standard remains widely referenced in compliance documentation. Requirement numbering reorganized substantially in v4.0 — mappings against v3.2.1 IDs do not transfer directly to v4.0 IDs.

current_extraction.notes: Extracted in ~8 minutes total on the GPU box without --force_ocr (PCI DSS v3.2.1 has a clean text layer, same as v4.0.1). The parser walks marker's Table block tree to emit one row per requirement. v3.2.1 tables differ from v4.0.1: 3-column 'PCI DSS Requirements / Testing Procedures / Guidance' (no Defined/Customized Approach distinction), and each Table block contains many requirements as separate rows.

extraction_history.reason: Initial extraction from UWO-mirrored PDF (PCI SSC removed v3.2.1 from direct download in 2024)

scope.extraction_notes: A3.1.3 and A3.2.2.1 are present in the PCI SSC source PDF but not captured as separate rows: marker rendered those table-continuations without the leading 'A3 Requirements' header column, so the parser cannot anchor a requirement ID. The testing-procedure text for both still appears under the preceding requirement.

notes: v3.2.1 is retired (superseded by v4.0/v4.0.1). Useful for mapping work that pre-dates the v4.0 cutover and for understanding the evolution of PCI DSS requirement numbering.
